titleStmt.title: Letter from T West to Lady Clonbrock, 22 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: T West

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from a prisoner of war, T West, Connaught Rangers, to Lady Clonbrock (1839-1928). West was incarcerated in Limburg, Germany at the time of the letter. He writes to Lady Clonbrock to thank her for the parcel she sent him, assuring her that 'the contents were thoroughly enjoyed by me.'Augusta Caroline Dillon (née Crofton) was the wife of Luke Gerald Dillon (1834-1917), the 4th Baron of Clonbrock, Co. Galway and the daughter of Lord Crofton of Mote Park (Edward Henry Churchill Crofton, 3rd Baron), Co. Roscommon. Aged 75 at the outbreak of war, Lady Clonbrock, worked closely with the Irish Women's Association to send basic necessities to Irish POWs. Many of her care packages went to members of the Connaught Rangers imprisoned in Limburg near Cologne.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Kriegsgefangenenlager Limburg, 22nd Feb 1916 Dear Lady Clonbrock Just a card thanking you
sincerely for your very kind
parcel which arrived quite
safe & I may say the contents
were thoroughly enjoyed by me. Again thanking you I remain
Yours Sincerely T.West Ink not allowed.
Gefangenenlager
Limburg (Lahn).
This is my address: Prisoner of War Name: T. West Conn. Rangers II. Bat. 4 Comp. No. Prisoner of War Limburg (Lahn) Germany Right side Feldpostkarte.
Kriegsgefangenensendung
Adresse: The Lady Clonbrock Clonbrock, Ahascragh, Co.Galway Ireland.
